name: MITRE.LIN.T1552.004.RULE Unsecured Credentials: Private Keys
notes:
The following auditd rules should be enabled:

-w /home/<user>/.ssh/ -p r -k siem-private-keys
-w /etc/ssl/certs/ -p r -k siem-private-keys
-w /etc/pki/ca-trust/extracted/pem/ -p r -k siem-private-keys
-w /etc/pki/tls/ -p -r -k siem-private-keys

Add your folders and files for certificates and keys.

Get more Linux MITRE rules: https://www.scnsoft.com/services/security/siem/linux-mitre-attack-rules
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
when the event matches Linux Auditd Key (custom) is any of siem-private-keys
when any of Linux UID (custom) are contained in any of MITRE: Linux Users - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
TD_list: APPLY MITRE.LIN.T1552.004.RULE Unsecured Credentials: Private Keys on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
AND when the event matches Linux Auditd Key (custom) is any of siem-private-keys
AND NOT when any of Linux UID (custom) are contained in any of MITRE: Linux Users - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
RED_list: Event name: MITRE.LIN.T1552.004.RULE Unsecured Credentials: Private Keys

Event description: Adversaries may gather private keys from compromised systems for use in authenticating to Remote Services like SSH or for use in decrypting other collected files such as email. Common key and certificate file extensions include: .key, .pgp, .gpg, .ppk., .p12, .pem, .pfx, .cer, .p7b, .asc. 
See more on: https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1552/004
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
Include detected events by "Source IP" from this point forward, in the offense, for 0 second(s)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
